title: Failure Analysis Report - Tipping mechanism jam on Wagon Tippler (RMHP-001)
failure_description: On 2024-08-05, the Wagon Tippler (RMHP-001) in the Raw Material Handling Plant section experienced a failure event characterized by tipping mechanism jam. The equipment was taken out of service for approximately 13.3 hours, resulting in an estimated production loss of 33.34 tonnes.

five_why_chain: Why did the failure occur? Tipping mechanism jam was observed.
Why did tipping mechanism jam occur? Investigation traced this to vibration/imbalance.
Why did vibration/imbalance occur? Inadequate monitoring/maintenance interval allowed the condition to progress undetected.
Why was monitoring/maintenance inadequate? Existing PM schedule and condition monitoring thresholds did not capture the early degradation signature for this failure mode.
Why were thresholds inadequate? Sensor alarm limits and PM frequency were based on generic OEM recommendations and had not been tuned using site-specific operating history.

corrective_actions.long_term: Implement additional condition monitoring sensor on affected component

symptoms_observed: Rising bearing/winding temperature trend over preceding days

five_why_chain: Why did the failure occur? Drive motor fault was observed.
Why did drive motor fault occur? Investigation traced this to insulation degradation.
Why did insulation degradation occur? Inadequate monitoring/maintenance interval allowed the condition to progress undetected.
Why was monitoring/maintenance inadequate? Existing PM schedule and condition monitoring thresholds did not capture the early degradation signature for this failure mode.
Why were thresholds inadequate? Sensor alarm limits and PM frequency were based on generic OEM recommendations and had not been tuned using site-specific operating history.

corrective_actions.long_term: Revise PM frequency based on condition monitoring trend data
